---
title: "Task 1: Geocoding Office Data"
subtitle: "Whole US Implementation"
author:
  - name: "William Clinton Co"
    email: "clinton.will.c@gmail.com"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
abstract: |
  This report summarizes the geocoding pipeline for CoStar commercial real estate lease data. The pipeline standardizes addresses and submits them to the U.S. Census Bureau Batch Geocoder API to obtain latitude, longitude, census tract, and FIPS codes. We first validated the approach on San Francisco (9,352 properties, 96% match rate), then scaled it to the entire U.S. dataset of 1,051,219 properties, achieving a 92.96% geocoding success rate. We additionally evaluated ZIP code matching as a post-geocoding validation step and found it does not materially improve outcomes.

format:
  pdf:
    fig-pos: "H"
    documentclass: article
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{float}
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
---


# This Week's Progress

The geocoding pipeline is now complete for the entire U.S. dataset. All 1,051,219 unique commercial properties from the CoStar lease database have been processed through address standardization, Census API geocoding, and ZIP code validation. The pipeline ran across 11 batches to stay within file size constraints and Census API limits.

Earlier in the project, we piloted the approach on San Francisco, achieving a 96% geocoding match rate across 9,352 properties. We then met to align on standardization methodology and confirmed our approach was consistent with reference code. The SF pilot confirmed the pipeline was ready for national scale.

# Address Standardization

The first step was to confirm we are using the same standardization process as reference code (Max's Code). After reviewing the reference, our standardization was determined to be an improvement.

Our standardization procedure converts addresses to uppercase, removes punctuation (except commas), abbreviates street types (e.g., STREET to ST, AVENUE to AVE), and collapses address ranges (e.g., "1234-5678 Main St" becomes "1234 Main St"). This format matches what the Census Batch Geocoder expects.

# ZIP Code Matching as a Validation Strategy

After geocoding, we evaluated ZIP code matching as a post-geocoding validation step. The idea was to compare the input ZIP code (from CoStar) against the ZIP code returned by the Census geocoder, and use agreement as a signal of match quality.

## San Francisco Results

@tbl-sf-zip-match shows the ZIP match breakdown for the SF pilot (9,352 properties).

| zip_match_detail | rows | pct |
| --- | --- | --- |
| One-to-One Matched | 8,937 | 95.56 |
| One-to-One Did Not Match | 42 | 0.45 |
| One-to-Many Matched Exactly One Output | 0 | 0.00 |
| One-to-Many Matched Multiple Outputs | 12 | 0.13 |
| One-to-Many Matched No Output | 6 | 0.06 |
| Geocoder ZIP Missing | 354 | 3.79 |
| Original ZIP Missing | 1 | 0.01 |

: San Francisco ZIP Match Detail Counts {#tbl-sf-zip-match}

## Whole U.S. Results

@tbl-whole-zip-match shows the same breakdown scaled to the full U.S. dataset (1,051,219 properties).

| zip_match_detail | rows | pct |
| --- | --- | --- |
| One-to-One Matched | 886,635 | 84.34 |
| One-to-One Did Not Match | 6,977 | 0.66 |
| One-to-Many Matched Exactly One Output | 15 | 0.00 |
| One-to-Many Matched Multiple Outputs | 5,969 | 0.57 |
| One-to-Many Matched No Output | 306 | 0.03 |
| Geocoder ZIP Missing | 67,721 | 6.44 |
| Original ZIP Missing | 83,596 | 7.95 |

: Whole U.S. ZIP Match Detail Counts {#tbl-whole-zip-match}

## Assessment

ZIP-based matching does not meaningfully improve geocoding outcomes. Even among records that appear to match at first glance, the approach introduces additional complexity, extra columns requiring flagging and review. In one-to-many cases with multiple outputs, we still observe a single input ZIP mapping to multiple candidate ZIPs or to incorrect ZIPs. The high rate of missing ZIP codes (6.44% from the geocoder, 7.95% from the original data) further limits the utility of this approach. A census-tract matching strategy is also unlikely to materially improve outcomes for the same reasons.

# Overall Match Statistics

@tbl-overall-summary shows the geocoding match statistics across the entire U.S. dataset.

| Metric | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Total Properties | 1,051,219 |
| Matched | 970,482 |
| Tied (Multiple Candidates) | 6,697 |
| Matched or Tied | 977,179 |
| Strict Match Rate | 92.32% |
| Matched-or-Tied Rate | 92.96% |

: Whole U.S. Geocoding Overall Summary {#tbl-overall-summary}

@tbl-match-type shows the breakdown by match type.

| match_type | rows | pct |
| --- | --- | --- |
| One-to-One | 1,044,522 | 99.36 |
| One-to-Many (Tie) | 6,697 | 0.64 |

: Whole U.S. Match Type Counts {#tbl-match-type}

@fig-state-map shows the strict match rate geographically. @tbl-state-summary shows the full breakdown by state, sorted by strict match rate descending.

![Census Geocoder Strict Match Rate by State](../../output/2_analysis/figures/whole_geocoder_state_match_rate_map.png){#fig-state-map width=100%}

| State | Total | Matched | Tied | Strict Match Rate (%) | Match-or-Tie Rate (%) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Rhode Island | 3,667 | 3,654 | 13 | 99.65 | 100.00 |
| New Hampshire | 3,782 | 3,768 | 14 | 99.63 | 100.00 |
| Massachusetts | 21,281 | 21,199 | 82 | 99.61 | 100.00 |
| Connecticut | 14,131 | 14,075 | 56 | 99.60 | 100.00 |
| Vermont | 1,101 | 1,096 | 5 | 99.55 | 100.00 |
| Maine | 2,975 | 2,957 | 18 | 99.39 | 100.00 |
| New Jersey | 30,168 | 29,957 | 211 | 99.30 | 100.00 |
| District of Columbia | 3,291 | 3,187 | 3 | 96.84 | 96.93 |
| Kansas | 7,596 | 7,296 | 32 | 96.05 | 96.47 |
| Alaska | 1,058 | 1,016 | 3 | 96.03 | 96.31 |
| California | 165,793 | 157,804 | 656 | 95.18 | 95.58 |
| Washington | 23,906 | 22,746 | 132 | 95.15 | 95.70 |
| Arizona | 24,670 | 23,373 | 60 | 94.74 | 94.99 |
| Colorado | 22,600 | 21,368 | 81 | 94.55 | 94.91 |
| Oregon | 14,913 | 14,093 | 81 | 94.50 | 95.04 |
| Michigan | 31,221 | 29,496 | 151 | 94.47 | 94.96 |
| Idaho | 5,704 | 5,388 | 32 | 94.46 | 95.02 |
| Ohio | 37,188 | 35,120 | 213 | 94.44 | 95.01 |
| Nevada | 11,878 | 11,194 | 50 | 94.24 | 94.66 |
| Nebraska | 4,953 | 4,666 | 17 | 94.21 | 94.55 |
| Iowa | 5,690 | 5,356 | 49 | 94.13 | 94.99 |
| South Dakota | 1,602 | 1,502 | 10 | 93.76 | 94.38 |
| Tennessee | 19,188 | 17,968 | 184 | 93.64 | 94.60 |
| Maryland | 18,351 | 17,116 | 77 | 93.27 | 93.69 |
| Virginia | 25,455 | 23,723 | 79 | 93.20 | 93.51 |
| North Carolina | 34,365 | 31,982 | 275 | 93.07 | 93.87 |
| Illinois | 39,768 | 36,959 | 310 | 92.94 | 93.72 |
| Indiana | 17,857 | 16,587 | 156 | 92.89 | 93.76 |
| Minnesota | 13,224 | 12,239 | 113 | 92.55 | 93.41 |
| Wisconsin | 13,646 | 12,598 | 70 | 92.32 | 92.83 |
| Montana | 1,959 | 1,807 | 24 | 92.24 | 93.47 |
| Utah | 10,044 | 9,256 | 56 | 92.15 | 92.71 |
| Delaware | 2,954 | 2,714 | 18 | 91.88 | 92.48 |
| Missouri | 17,780 | 16,327 | 179 | 91.83 | 92.83 |
| Oklahoma | 12,095 | 11,099 | 66 | 91.77 | 92.31 |
| Florida | 79,864 | 73,271 | 809 | 91.74 | 92.76 |
| New Mexico | 5,291 | 4,840 | 48 | 91.48 | 92.38 |
| South Carolina | 19,076 | 17,431 | 180 | 91.38 | 92.32 |
| Kentucky | 9,212 | 8,418 | 78 | 91.38 | 92.23 |
| Arkansas | 6,428 | 5,872 | 45 | 91.35 | 92.05 |
| Louisiana | 10,206 | 9,275 | 79 | 90.88 | 91.65 |
| Wyoming | 722 | 656 | 3 | 90.86 | 91.27 |
| Pennsylvania | 37,662 | 34,021 | 213 | 90.33 | 90.90 |
| Georgia | 36,736 | 33,120 | 479 | 90.16 | 91.46 |
| New York | 62,311 | 56,172 | 354 | 90.15 | 90.72 |
| Texas | 88,171 | 77,391 | 556 | 87.77 | 88.40 |
| North Dakota | 1,748 | 1,517 | 31 | 86.78 | 88.56 |
| Alabama | 11,378 | 9,789 | 141 | 86.03 | 87.27 |
| Mississippi | 4,320 | 3,655 | 77 | 84.61 | 86.39 |
| West Virginia | 2,724 | 2,114 | 17 | 77.61 | 78.23 |
| Hawaii | 3,197 | 2,254 | 11 | 70.50 | 70.85 |

: Match Rate by State {#tbl-state-summary}


# Appendix {.appendix}

Link to code repository:

- https://github.com/WilliamClintC/Geocoding_Office
